title: Find your starting point
intro: A short European Portuguese check that gets harder as it goes, so we can start you in the right place. It takes about two minutes and you can retake it any time.

questions.prompt: 'Eu ___ portuguesa.' (I am Portuguese)
questions.explanation: With eu, the verb ser is sou.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'good morning'?
questions.explanation: Bom dia is used before lunchtime; boa tarde and boa noite come later in the day.

questions.prompt: Someone thanks you. What do you reply?
questions.explanation: De nada is the standard reply to thanks, like 'you're welcome'.

questions.prompt: 'A sopa ___ fria hoje.' (the soup is cold today, a temporary state)
questions.explanation: A temporary state takes estar, while a permanent quality takes ser.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'it's half past three'?
questions.explanation: Hours from two onwards take the plural são, and half past is e meia.

questions.prompt: '___ problema é difícil.' (this word looks feminine but is not)
questions.explanation: Problema is masculine despite the final a, so it takes o.

questions.prompt: Present tense: 'A loja ___ às nove da manhã.' (abrir)
questions.explanation: An -IR verb with a singular subject takes the ending -e: abre.

questions.prompt: In a café in Portugal you want a small black espresso. What do you order?
questions.explanation: Um café is the small espresso; galão and meia de leite are milky coffees.

questions.prompt: '___ é que tu moras?' (asking about the place)
questions.explanation: Onde asks about place; quando asks about time.

questions.prompt: Completed past: 'Ontem eu ___ um filme.' (ver)
questions.explanation: One finished event in the past takes the pretérito perfeito simples: vi.

questions.prompt: Replace the object in 'Comprei o bilhete.' with a pronoun.
questions.explanation: The direct object pronoun o attaches after the verb with a hyphen: comprei-o.

questions.prompt: You have finished eating in a restaurant and want to pay. What do you ask for?
questions.explanation: A conta is the bill; a ementa is the menu and a gorjeta is the tip.

questions.prompt: Past habit: 'Quando era pequeno, ___ à bola todos os dias.' (jogar)
questions.explanation: A repeated habit in the past takes the imperfeito: jogava.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct for 'They told me nothing'?
questions.explanation: A negative word pulls the pronoun in front of the verb: não me disseram.

questions.prompt: At the doctor you want to say that your throat hurts.
questions.explanation: Garganta is the throat; estômago, cabeça and joelho are other body parts.

questions.prompt: Telling a friend the way: '___ sempre em frente até ao mercado.' (seguir, tu)
questions.explanation: The tu imperative drops the final s of the present form, and siga is the formal one.

questions.prompt: 'Este restaurante é ___ do que o outro.' (better)
questions.explanation: Bom has the irregular comparative melhor, never mais bom.

questions.prompt: Which word means 'bus' in Portugal?
questions.explanation: Portugal uses autocarro; ónibus is Brazilian and elétrico is the tram.

questions.prompt: 'Quero que tu ___ cedo.' (vir)
questions.explanation: Querer que triggers the presente do conjuntivo: que tu venhas.

questions.prompt: 'Ele prometeu que ___ o dinheiro na sexta.' (devolver, standard written form)
questions.explanation: A future seen from a past moment takes the condicional: devolveria.

questions.prompt: How do you politely ask an older stranger in a shop if she wants a bag?
questions.explanation: European Portuguese prefers o senhor or a senhora with strangers, not tu or você.

questions.prompt: 'Procuro alguém que ___ falar alemão.' (saber, the person may not exist)
questions.explanation: A relative clause about an unidentified person takes the conjuntivo: saiba.

questions.prompt: He asked: 'Tens tempo?' Report it: 'Ele perguntou-me ___ eu tinha tempo.'
questions.explanation: A reported yes or no question is introduced by se.

questions.prompt: 'O autor ___ livro li é português.' (whose)
questions.explanation: Cujo means whose and agrees with the thing possessed, here livro.

questions.prompt: Combine the pronouns me and o: 'He gave me the book.'
questions.explanation: Me plus o contract into mo, so the verb takes deu-mo.

questions.prompt: A sign in an estate agent's window meaning 'houses for sale'.
questions.explanation: In the passive with se the verb agrees with the plural noun and the pronoun follows it.

questions.prompt: 'Ela insistiu ___ pagar a conta.'
questions.explanation: Insistir governs the preposition em before an infinitive.
